qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated REST API exposes full configuration including database passwords and API tokens
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets unauthenticated REST endpoints that return Glances configuration data. The advisory targets SQL injection in glances/exports/glances_timescaledb/__init__.py where normalize() fails to escape single quotes before cur.execute(); the vulnerable code, entrypoint, source-to-sink path, and root cause differ.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described TimescaleDB export SQL injection root cause: unescaped single quotes in normalize() values concatenated into INSERT statements and executed with cur.execute().
evidence: Advisory GHSA-x46r-mf5g-xpr6 describes SQL injection in glances/exports/glances_timescaledb/__init__.py through normalize() and direct INSERT query execution.
The judge input manifest lists one final qualifying finding: unauthenticated_config_disclosure.

The unauthenticated_config_disclosure report describes GET /api/4/config and GET /api/4/args exposing configuration and CLI arguments without authentication, not TimescaleDB SQL query construction or process-name injection.
